message.content:
Find all files related to certifications in this portfolio project. Look for:
1. Components that render certifications
2. Data files that define certifications
3. The sidebar component structure
4. Any modal/lightbox/view components

Search thoroughly for files containing "certification", "cert", "sidebar", "experience". Report file paths, line numbers, and key code snippets.

content.thinking: I now have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report.

content.text:
Here is my complete report on all certification-related files, sidebar components, and modal/view components in this portfolio project. --- ## 1. Certification Data File **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\data\resume.ts`** (lines 78-169) This is the primary data source. It defines 15 certification entries inside the `resumeData` object under the `certifications` array. Each entry conforms to the `CertificationItem` type. Key snippet (lines 78-84): ```ts certifications: [ { id: 'cs50-1', name: "CS50's Web Programming with Python and JavaScript", issuer: 'HarvardX / CS50', issueDate: new Date('2024-03-15'), }, // ... 14 more entries ] ``` The data is destructured at line 17 of `content.tsx`: ```ts const { personalInfo, summary, experience, education, skills, certifications } = resumeData; ``` --- ## 2. Certification Type Definition **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\types\index.ts`** (lines 77-86) ```ts export interface CertificationItem { id: string; name: string; issuer: string; issueDate: Date; expiryDate?: Date; credentialId?: string; credentialUrl?: string; imageUrl?: string; } ``` Note: The actual data in `resume.ts` only uses `id`, `name`, `issuer`, and `issueDate`. The optional fields (`expiryDate`, `credentialId`, `credentialUrl`, `imageUrl`) are not populated for any of the 15 certifications. The `CertificationModal` component has conditional rendering for `imageUrl` (line 101) and `credentialId` (line 139), but these will never render since the data lacks these fields. --- ## 3. Certification Rendering Components ### 3a. CertificationModal (detailed view) **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\CertificationModal.tsx`** (157 lines) This is a full-screen modal overlay that shows details of a single certification when clicked. Key features: - **Props** (lines 9-13): `isOpen`, `onClose`, `certification` (of type `CertificationItem | null`) - **Escape key handling** (line 20): closes on Escape key - **Body scroll lock** (line 29): sets `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'` when open - **Backdrop click** (lines 38-41): closes when clicking outside the modal content - **Image rendering** (lines 101-118): conditionally renders `certification.imageUrl` using Next.js `Image` -- but no certifications currently have `imageUrl` set - **Credential ID** (lines 139-147): conditionally renders `certification.credentialId` in monospace font -- also not populated in data - **Always displays**: `certification.name` (line 121), `certification.issuer` (line 127), `certification.issueDate` formatted as "Month Year" (line 135) ### 3b. Certification cards in Experience section **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\data\content.tsx`** (lines 525-554) The `ExperienceSection` function (defined at line 454) renders certification cards as clickable buttons in a 2-column grid. Key snippet (lines 532-553): ```tsx {certifications.map((cert, index) => ( openCertModal(cert)} // ... className="text-left p-4 bg-slate-800/80 backdrop-blur-sm rounded-xl border ... hover:border-orange-500 ..." >
{cert.name}

{cert.issuer}

 ))} ``` Each card shows: a CheckCircle icon, the certification name (clamped to 2 lines), the issuer, and an ExternalLink icon. Clicking calls `openCertModal(cert)`. ### 3c. Certifications in ResumeModal **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\ResumeModal.tsx`** (lines 198-226) The resume modal also lists certifications when `resumeContent.certifications.length > 0`. It renders a 2-column grid of cards showing `cert.name`, `cert.issuer`, issue/expiry dates, and a "View Credential" link (only if `credentialUrl` is set, which none currently are). --- ## 4. Sidebar Component Structure ### 4a. Left Sidebar **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\Sidebar.tsx`** (45 lines) A composition of three child components: 1. **UserProfile** (line 20-24) -- shows name, title, profile photo, resume button, contact info 2. **NavigationMenu** (line 28-31) -- section nav links 3. **ThemeToggle** (line 36) -- dark/light mode toggle + footer text Props interface at `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\types\components.ts` lines 5-9: ```ts export interface SidebarProps { currentSection: NavigationSection; onSectionChange: (section: NavigationSection) => void; onResumeOpen: () => void; } ``` ### 4b. UserProfile **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\UserProfile.tsx`** (107 lines) Renders profile image (theme-aware: `black.png` or `white.png`), name, title, a "Resume" button (calls `onResumeClick`), and contact details (email, phone, location). ### 4c. NavigationMenu **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\NavigationMenu.tsx`** (73 lines) Defines 7 navigation items at lines 9-17: ```ts const navigationItems = [ { section: 'home', label: 'Home', icon: }, { section: 'about', label: 'About', icon: }, { section: 'skills', label: 'Skills', icon: }, { section: 'experience', label: 'Experience', icon: }, { section: 'projects', label: 'Projects', icon: }, { section: 'github', label: 'GitHub', icon: }, { section: 'contact', label: 'Contact', icon: }, ]; ``` The "Experience" nav item (with `Briefcase` icon) navigates to the section that renders work experience, education, AND certifications together. ### 4d. Right Sidebar **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\RightSidebar.tsx`** (109 lines) A static sidebar showing: - **Core Stack** (lines 51-66): 12 skill badges with icons - **Expertise** (lines 69-87): 6 expertise areas - **Connect** (lines 89-106): GitHub and LinkedIn social links --- ## 5. Modal / Overlay Components ### 5a. CertificationModal **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\CertificationModal.tsx`** -- described above in section 3a. ### 5b. ResumeModal **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\ResumeModal.tsx`** (233 lines) A full-screen modal showing the complete resume. Sections rendered: - Personal info (line 98-108) - Professional Summary (lines 111-114) - Experience (lines 116-148) - Skills (lines 151-167) - Education (lines 169-196) - Certifications (lines 198-226) -- conditional on array length > 0 Same escape-key / backdrop-click pattern as CertificationModal. --- ## 6. State Management for Certifications ### 6a. NavigationContext **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\contexts\NavigationContext.tsx`** (61 lines) Certification-related state (lines 11-12, 26-34): ```ts const [selectedCertification, setSelectedCertification] = useState(null); const [isCertModalOpen, setIsCertModalOpen] = useState(false); const openCertModal = useCallback((cert: CertificationItem) => { setSelectedCertification(cert); setIsCertModalOpen(true); }, []); const closeCertModal = useCallback(() => { setIsCertModalOpen(false); setSelectedCertification(null); }, []); ``` ### 6b. NavigationContextType **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\types\index.ts`** (lines 6-15) and duplicated at **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\types\context.ts`** (lines 3-15): ```ts selectedCertification: CertificationItem | null; isCertModalOpen: boolean; openCertModal: (cert: CertificationItem) => void; closeCertModal: () => void; ``` --- ## 7. Page-Level Wiring **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\app\page.tsx`** (121 lines) The `PortfolioContent` component (line 17) pulls all navigation state via `useNavigation()` and renders: - Left Sidebar (lines 39-50) - Main content area with `ContentArea` (lines 53-74) - Right Sidebar (lines 77-84) - ResumeModal (lines 88-96) -- conditionally rendered - CertificationModal (lines 98-107) -- conditionally rendered when `isCertModalOpen && selectedCertification` The `getSectionContent` function (from `content.tsx`) receives `openCertModal` as a callback parameter so the experience section can trigger the modal. --- ## 8. Component Exports **`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\index.ts`** (line 11): ```ts export { CertificationModal } from './CertificationModal'; ``` --- ## Summary of the Certification Data Flow 1. **Data** is defined in `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\data\resume.ts` as an array of 15 `CertificationItem` objects 2. **Type** is defined in `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\types\index.ts` as `CertificationItem` 3. **State** for the selected certification and modal open/close is managed in `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\contexts\NavigationContext.tsx` 4. **Cards** are rendered in the Experience section at `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\data\content.tsx` (lines 525-554) as clickable buttons 5. **Modal** is rendered at `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\CertificationModal.tsx` when a card is clicked 6. **Resume modal** also lists certifications at `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\ResumeModal.tsx` (lines 198-226) 7. **Page** wires everything together at `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\app\page.tsx` Key observation: The `CertificationItem` type supports `imageUrl`, `credentialId`, `credentialUrl`, and `expiryDate` fields, and the `CertificationModal` has conditional rendering for them, but none of the 15 certifications in the data currently use these optional fields.
